text:
PARIS 芒聙聰 There has been plenty of talk about the waves created by theshallower-than-normal Olympic pool. But on the final night of the swimming competition here, the pool created something different芒聙聰emotional whiplash for the Americans.

Bobby Finke芒聙聶s all-guts world record in the 1,500-meter freestyle was a massively clutch swim that kept alive the U.S. streak of at least one individual men芒聙聶s gold medal in the pool since 1900. It was followed by a sobering, first-time-in-Olympic-history loss for the U.S. men芒聙聶s medley relay, with the dethroning delivered by a Chinese team dogged by doping controversies. And then that gave way to a powerful, walk-off world record by the American women芒聙聶s medley relay.

Fin, as they say in French.

Now the reckoning begins.

The final day was an appropriate mixed bag of results for a wildly uneven, largely disappointing U.S. performance. USA Swimming can point to winning both the gold medal and overall medal tallies, with eight golds and 28 total medals to Australia芒聙聶s seven golds and 18 total medals. After losing the gold-medal tally 13-7 to the Aussies at World Championships last year,脗聽 that芒聙聶s at least one embarrassment reversed. Barely.

But here芒聙聶s the bigger picture: eight golds is the fewest by the Americans in the pool since 1988, and the 28 total is the fewest since 2004. And keep in mind, three events and nine medal chances have been added since 2020.

Nobody expected this to be an easy swimming Olympics for the country that has historically dominated. But hopes were raised after a fast performance at U.S. Olympic trials in June in Indianapolis, and then the Americans failed to follow through in France.

Times are difficult to compare from Indy to now because of the La D脙漏fense Arena pool, which produced a lot ofslow performances and some erratic results. Place matters more than time at the Olympics anyway, and in general the U.S. did not swim up to its seeding.

Across 35 events, American swimmers finished a combined 89 places lower than their seeding on the pre-meet entry list芒聙聰an average drop of 2.5 spots. The women fared considerably better than the men in that regard, while also winning five golds to the men芒聙聶s two (one was shared in the mixed medley relay).

A few extreme busts skew the seeding number slightly, when other metrics make the overall performance look slightly less drastic. For instance, five times Americans were seeded to win medals and missed the podium, while three Americans took home hardware who were not expected to, according to seeding.

Still, the overall picture of underperformance is pretty clear, and the sky-high U.S. standards don芒聙聶t allow for much in the way of underperforming. Swimming might only be second to basketball in terms of the public demand to dominate at the Olympics.

American male headliner Caeleb Dressel, who bounced back from a two-event debacle Friday to swim a very good butterfly leg on the medley relay, spoke two truths that are in conflict with each other: the standards aren芒聙聶t changing in the U.S. and the world is catching up.

芒聙聹It wasn芒聙聶t a bad meet at all,芒聙聺 said Dressel, who has won nine Olympic medals, eight of them gold. 芒聙聹The goal when you come to the Olympics and World Championships is gold medals. There芒聙聶s other countries that are getting very dominant. The wealth has just been spread around. I don芒聙聶t think we芒聙聶re getting any worse, per se.脗

芒聙聹I don芒聙聶t think the goals need to change because the world is getting faster.脗聽 I don芒聙聶t think we start striving for silver and bronze芒聙聰not that there芒聙聶s anything wrong with that, but we want to be the most dominant team. And we were at these Games.芒聙聺

The margins were just smaller. And truth be told, the U.S. went into the weekend needing to swim very well to salvage anything positive from this meet.

That largely happened. On SaturdayKatie Ledecky did what she does, winning the 800 freestyle, and then the mixed medley relay came through. And on Sunday, Finke and the women芒聙聶s relay sandwiched some heroics around another otherworldly Chinese performance.

Leading up to his race, Finke was well aware ofAmerica芒聙聶s no-gold individual performance by the men. Not one to shy away from what many athletes would call 芒聙聹outside noise芒聙聺 he said he actually dove into the comments section on the niche website Swimswam.com.

芒聙聹I like reading that stuff,芒聙聺 Finke said. 芒聙聹It kind of motivates me inside.芒聙聺

That said, Finke also acknowledged feeling pressure. After winning both the 800 and 1,500 in Tokyo three years ago, he couldn芒聙聶t quite rally to catch Ireland芒聙聶s Daniel Wiffen in the shorter of the two races earlier in the week. So he got on the blocks for the 1,500 and just went for it.

That芒聙聶s a scary strategy in the longest pool race, one that can come at a heavy cost. And it runs counter to Finke芒聙聶s trademark, late-charging style. But with everything on the line he just tossed aside caution and attacked from the very first stroke.

芒聙聹That really was not my strategy going into the race,芒聙聺 he acknowledged with a laugh. 芒聙聹I didn芒聙聶t know how the race was going to play out. I kind of saw I had a pretty decent lead at the 300 so I knew I had to keep going and make the guys hurt a little bit. They started to catch up to me and I was getting a little worried, so I knew I had to keep pushing.芒聙聺

Wiffen and, more pertinently, Italy芒聙聶s Gregorio Paltrinieri, were the ones trying to catch Finke. While his lead was getting smaller, his pace ahead of world-record time was getting larger. The crowd got behind Finke芒聙聶s record pursuit in the latter stages.

But Finke was only concerned with finishing first, trying to complete the transformation from a dead closer to a front-runner.

芒聙聹I can芒聙聶t let go of this now,芒聙聺 he thought to himself. 芒聙聹I can芒聙聶t be the guy who got run down after I do all the running down.芒聙聺

He ran away instead, scoring the last 50 in trademark Finke fashion to break the 12-year-old world record of China芒聙聶s Sun Yang. In the whack-a-mole game of China and drug testing, removing the controversial Sun from the record books芒聙聰he芒聙聶s served a drug suspension and been involved in testing brouhahas芒聙聰was followed minutes later by that country winning the men芒聙聶s medley.

The race played out as a taut drama between the Chinese, Americans and French, to the roaring delight of the fans.脗

The Americans went with the quartet of Ryan Murphy, Nic Fink, Caeleb Dressel and Hunter Armstrong and it almost paid off. Murphy and Fink had good opening splits, but not quite as fast as they were Saturday on the front end of the mixed medley芒聙聰they were a combined 1.04 seconds slower, which loomed large in a race decided by .55 seconds.

Dressel had a monster fly leg, clocking a 49.41, but that didn芒聙聶t provide quite enough cushion for Armstrong going up against China芒聙聶s Pan Zhanle, who smashed his own world record in the 100 free earlier in the meetin an eyebrow-raising 46.40 seconds. Pan followed that up with what might be the fastest relay split in human history, dropping a 45.92 to win the race芒聙聰that芒聙聶s .14 faster than Jason Lezak芒聙聶s immortal 100 free split for the U.S. in the 2008 Olympics that kept Michael Phelps芒聙聶 drive for eight gold medals alive.

Given China芒聙聶s drug history, this was not an overwhelmingly popular result. Twenty-three swimmers had positive tests for Trimetazidine in 2021,which was revealed in the spring, and there have been revelations about other positive tests that were kept secret and yielded no penalties. Eleven Chinese swimmers at the Olympics were among those who tested positive in 芒聙聶21, and two of those 11 were on the winning relay.

Pan, it should be noted, was not one of them. But his times have been a source of great discussion. Without sounding at all accusatory, Dressel said Pan芒聙聶s 100 freestyle world record swim here was one of two in modern times that made his 芒聙聹jaw drop,芒聙聺 with the other being Adam Peaty芒聙聶s 100 breastroke world record in 2020.

The question was asked of the American relay about losing to China, and Fink stepped in to answer it for Dressel. He answered about shortcomings by the World Anti-Doping Agency (WADA) in general, not China.

芒聙聹It芒聙聶s questions about the system,芒聙聺 Fink said. 芒聙聹Is WADA doing everything it can? We want more clarity and transparency on all this stuff. Nothing against the athletes competing, it芒聙聶s just questions about the system. It seems like there芒聙聶s cycle after cycle of concerns and questions.芒聙聺

Following that race, the American women finished an overall positive meet with a thunderclap of a relay. Backstroker Regan Smith, breastroker Lilly King, butterflyer Gretchen Walsh and freestyler Torri Huske made sure it wasn芒聙聶t close, with King breaking it open and the lead growing from there.脗

It was a nice valedictory moment for King, who is in the last of her three Olympics. The other three figure to be mainstays of the American team through 2028.

The present and future on the women芒聙聶s side is bright. The men芒聙聶s side should improve after being caught in a bit of a generation gap in 2024芒聙聰there is a youth movement coming, some of which got valuable Olympic experience here.

芒聙聹Those guys are kind of getting a taste for it now, they芒聙聶re going to be in the hunt for gold, for sure,芒聙聺 Fink said. 芒聙聹We芒聙聶re excited for them.芒聙聺

For now, though, USA Swimming exits Paris with a mixed bag of results that didn芒聙聶t live up to the traditional standard. There will be changes ahead, because a mixed bag won芒聙聶t cut it at a home Olympics in 2028.脗

Pat Forde is a senior writer for Sports Illustrated who covers college football and college basketball as well as the Olympics and horse racing. He cohosts the College Football Enquirer podcast and is a football analyst on the Big Ten Network. He previously worked for Yahoo Sports, ESPN and The (Louisville) Courier-Journal. Forde has won 28 Associated Press Sports Editors writing contest awards, has been published three times in the Best American Sports Writing book series, and was nominated for the 1990 Pulitzer Prize. A past president of the U.S. Basketball Writers Association and member of the Football Writers Association of America, he lives in Louisville with his wife. They have three children, all of whom were collegiate swimmers.

title: Bobby Finke Saves a Century-Old Streak to Close Out Swimming at Paris Olympics
description: The women also won the 4X100 relay on Sunday, but despite the gold medals, the final result for USA swimming shows an overall picture of underperformance.
